svg.text: Import once, add incrementally, export any format on demand with full provenance
Import first batch with one command
All of this happens automatically during import:
• Filters applied (QUAL, depth, MAF, biallelic, regions) per your flags
• Genotypes packed into byte arrays (2 bits/sample)
• Population stats pre-computed (ac[], an[], af[] per population)
• Graph built: Variant, Sample, Population, Chromosome nodes + relationships
Annotations stored as graph edges with version tracking. Original data untouched.
Export any format with one command (no scripts needed)
records samples, filters, parameters, timestamp, GraphMana version, database state
Run analyses on exported files (same tools as before)
PCA, Fst, Admixture, TreeMix, demographic inference, selection scans...
New samples append to existing packed arrays.
Same filters applied to new data during import.
Existing annotations remain. Annotate new variants only.
Re-export any format on demand (same one-line commands).
ac[], an[], af[] recomputed for new sample set.
Collaborator wants African samples in PLINK
graphmana export --format plink --filter-maf 0.01 -o study_maf01 # change one flag
graphmana annotate --type clinvar --input clinvar_2024Q2.tsv --mode update --version Q2
Each: one graphmana ingest --mode incremental command. Minutes, not weeks.
All provenance is automatic. No extra effort from the user.
The same year: 0 merge cycles, 0 conversion scripts, automatic provenance on every export.
New batch? One command. New format? One command. New filters? Change one flag. Full audit trail? Built in.
Weeks of re-work become minutes of re-export.
